Definition: The weight of the diamond, measured in carats. One carat equals 0.2 grams.
A quality grade describing the cut of the diamond, affecting sparkle. Levels: Fair, Good, Very Good, Premium, Ideal.
A quality grade describing the diamond's color, with D (best/colorless) to J (more yellow tint).
Quality grade describing internal/external flaws. Categories: IF, VVS1, VVS2, VS1, VS2, SI1, SI2, I1.
Total depth as a percentage of average diameter, (z / mean(x, y)) * 100.
Width of table (flat top) as a percentage of average diameter, (table / mean(x, y)) * 100.
Length of the diamond in millimeters (mm).
Width of the diamond in millimeters (mm).
Depth (height) of the diamond in millimeters (mm).
Price of the diamond in US dollars.
